title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: Jack Roberts discusses the unexpected withdrawal of Claude Fable 5 and argues that users should run AI models locally to avoid dependency on cloud providers. He demonstrates running open-source models via Ollama and building a local NotebookLM alternative with model routing across Claude, DeepSeek, and other services.
writeup: The video responds to the withdrawal of Claude Fable 5 by making the case for local inference as a hedge against dependency on cloud providers. Jack Roberts demonstrates running open-source models through Ollama and builds a local NotebookLM alternative that uses model routing across Claude, DeepSeek, and other services. The takeaway: keeping inference local and routing across multiple foundation models reduces exposure when a single hosted model is pulled.
